updated the filter to less than 3 lanes, and a speed limit less than 51MPH https://t.co/iZ1dD7aTOk
added some circles and forced labels to be horizontal https://t.co/czpkXmzvb3
greyscaled out wikimedia unlabeled basemap for a little more context https://t.co/KHah4xYocO
should probably try and get some labels for these roads so I know what I am looking at https://t.co/tjQFu5Aym4

In Zen and The Art of Motorcycle Maintenance, there's a good passage about how to spot good motorcycle routes on a map - I want to try to capture some of this, maybe make it a bit easier https://t.co/pi6rn3iTeI

okay I got distracted, what I really want to do is start working on a layer I can use to make maps of good motorcycle routes - to start I'm gonna eliminate big highways which I do not enjoy driving my tiny motorcycle on https://t.co/TcWwRYmNJv

I  don't like  the way the thicker (bigger, more-laned) roads block out the smaller roads, but it's pretty easy to fix the render order in qgis - just have to set it to render the roads with the least amount of lanes last, which I think makes it much easier to read dense areas https://t.co/5aZcFWTnzZ
playing around with the color scale for speed limits... I like the orange one best, I think https://t.co/8vxm7NHpVV
you can also make each road's thickness reflect how many lanes it  has (ie, more lanes makes a thicker road!) and things start to look more "right" almost immediately https://t.co/Ou1UTp1f9z
okay I am starting over, just color by speed limit to get things rolling https://t.co/dDvh4ATrMd

I am still trying to find the words for what I am intuiting, but I think its the difference between analyzing the nodes vs the links. Almost all modeling is done on the nodes, and very little is done on the (uncaptured, therefore invisible) linkages between them https://t.co/ZO38Ad8kVU
